summary: First testing run for HopeBridge Foundation NGO website. Executed 15 pytest backend tests (all passed) covering root endpoint, volunteers, contact, newsletter (with idempotency), donation checkout for all 3 packages, custom-amount validation (min/max), invalid package rejection, server-side price authoritativeness, and status endpoint. Executed Playwright frontend tests covering Hero headline & CTAs, Volunteer form submit + toast + form-clear, Nav links, Donate flow (friend package -> real Stripe checkout at ₹1,000.00), Contact form submit, Newsletter subscribe, and FAQ accordion. All backend APIs return 200 with correct schema, no MongoDB _id leak. Stripe integration works end-to-end via emergentintegrations.

minor.issue: Returns 200 instead of 201 for resource creation (still functional, minor REST semantics)
Returns 200 instead of 201 for resource creation
Returns 200 for both new subscription and existing (idempotent) - acceptable but could differentiate

issues: Navbar starts transparent over hero image; on scroll becomes white bg. When at top on some sub-pages the nav-donate button and nav links float over hero with reasonable contrast, works OK. No fix required unless main agent wants nav visible from load.

action_items: No blocking issues. Optional: return HTTP 201 from POST endpoints (volunteers/contact/newsletter) for REST correctness. Optional: differentiate new subscription vs existing subscription in newsletter response (e.g., include 'existing': bool).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is well-structured (~300 lines). No secrets hardcoded. Uses env vars, uuid ids, ObjectId excluded via projection {"_id": 0} in list/find_one - good.
Stripe amount is correctly server-authoritative: _resolve_amount() ignores custom_amount when package_id is provided. Verified via test_checkout_server_side_amount_authoritative.
Idempotency of newsletter subscription works (existing email returns same doc without inserting duplicate).
One minor concern: /api/donations/status/{session_id} instantiates a new StripeCheckout on every call - fine for now but could be cached.
All key interactive elements have data-testid attributes as promised in the review request.
test_credentials: N/A - no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_ prefixed volunteer/contact/newsletter records and initiated a few Stripe test checkout sessions during testing. Data is left in DB but does not affect functionality.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All endpoints and flows tested and green. Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py and can be re-run: `cd /app && REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://impact-bridge-org.preview.emergentagent.com python -m pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v`. Stripe integration uses emergentintegrations (STRIPE_API_KEY=sk_test_emergent) and real checkout URLs are generated (https://checkout.stripe.com/c/pay/cs_test_...). Do NOT complete a real payment; verifying redirect + 200 response is sufficient.
